title: crepis borealis
intro: Crepis borealis, also known as the Northern Hawk's-beard or Rough Hawk's-beard, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent due to its content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Crepis borealis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. Crepis borealis can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, with the dried roots and leaves typically used in herbal remedies.
